reason: Response B has the grounded, multi-part structure I tend toward: it names the feeling plainly, cites real words (saudade, hiraeth, jamais vu) with honest hedging about how they fall short, and lands on an interpersonal/kindness note rather than a performative flourish. Response A reads like an imitation reaching for "literary Claude" — it invents a neologism with a shaky etymology (velation derives from veiling, not unveiling) and closes with the showy line "I would use this word every day," a kind of self-satisfied coinage I'm more cautious about. B's tendency to over-deliver with extra bonus wishes and self-aware qualification feels more like my actual register.
